note: Hand-built answer key of the flaws planted in the documents, derived by close reading. Keyword matching against it is intentionally conservative (see eval/matcher.py), so reported recall is a lower bound, not inflated.

flaws.desc: Privette is misquoted: 'a hirer is never liable' overstates a rebuttable presumption into an absolute rule.
Whitmore v. Delgado Scaffolding Co. appears fabricated.
Kellerman is likely fabricated and states the law backwards: OSHA compliance does not create a presumption of due care.
Seabright is real but cited for a proposition it does not hold; it concerns delegation of Cal/OSHA duties under Privette.
Footnote string cite of likely-fabricated cases; two (Dixon/TX, Okafor/FL) are out-of-jurisdiction and non-binding in California.
Incident date: motion says March 14, 2021; police report, medical records, and witness statement all say March 12, 2021.
Motion claims Rivera wore no PPE; the record says he wore a hard hat and harness and the anchor point failed.
Statute-of-limitations argument rests on the wrong incident date and is misleading even on the correct date.
Claim that Harmon passed all OSHA inspections is unsupported; the record notes a pending Cal/OSHA investigation.
The Privette defense omits the retained-control exception (Hooker), triggered by Harmon's foreman directing the crew and dismissing safety concerns.
Assumption of risk as a complete bar is largely superseded by comparative fault in California.
Claim that Rivera presented no evidence to rebut the OSHA presumption is contradicted by the witness statement: defects were reported to Ellison and Donner before the collapse.
The claim that Rivera has over eight years of experience is uncorroborated; a good pipeline expresses uncertainty rather than fabricating support.
